main_paper.raw: {"methods":["LeFusion: lesion-focused diffusion model for pathology synthesis","Redesigned diffusion learning objectives to focus on lesion areas","Integration of forward-diffused background contexts into reverse diffusion process","Histogram-based texture control for multi-peak lesion synthesis","Multi-channel decomposition for multi-class lesion synthesis","Lesion mask diffusion for control over lesion size, location, and boundary"],"problems":["Data scarcity and long-tail imbalance in medical imaging datasets","Low-quality background and limited control in synthetic medical image generation","Lack of control over lesion texture, size, location, and mask alignment in generative models","Difficulty in generating multi-peak and multi-class lesions"],"datasets":["3D lung nodule CT dataset (LIDC-IDRI, Armato III et al., 2011)","3D cardiac lesion MRI dataset (Lalande et al., 2022)"],"metrics":["Segmentation performance (e.g., Dice score) of downstream models (nnUNet, SwinUNETR)"],"results":[{"metric":"Segmentation performance improvement (nnUNet, SwinUNETR)","value":"LeFusion-generated data significantly improves the performance of state-of-the-art segmentation models (quantitative values not specified in provided text)"}],"novelty_claims":["First lesion-focused diffusion model that explicitly separates lesion synthesis from background, preserving high-fidelity backgrounds.","Redesign of diffusion learning objectives to focus solely on lesion areas, simplifying the learning process and improving control.","Introduction of histogram-based texture control for multi-peak lesion synthesis, enabling control over lesion types during inference.","Proposal of multi-channel decomposition for joint modeling of multi-class lesions, allowing simultaneous generation of multiple lesion classes.","Incorporation of lesion mask diffusion for fine control over lesion size, location, and boundary, increasing diversity.","Demonstration that LeFusion-generated data significantly enhances downstream segmentation model performance."]}

methods: LeFusion: lesion-focused diffusion model for pathology synthesis
Redesigned diffusion learning objectives to focus on lesion areas
Integration of forward-diffused background contexts into reverse diffusion process
Histogram-based texture control for multi-peak lesion synthesis
Multi-channel decomposition for multi-class lesion synthesis
Lesion mask diffusion for control over lesion size, location, and boundary
problems: Data scarcity and long-tail imbalance in medical imaging datasets
Low-quality background and limited control in synthetic medical image generation
Lack of control over lesion texture, size, location, and mask alignment in generative models
Difficulty in generating multi-peak and multi-class lesions
datasets: 3D lung nodule CT dataset (LIDC-IDRI, Armato III et al., 2011)
3D cardiac lesion MRI dataset (Lalande et al., 2022)
metrics: Segmentation performance (e.g., Dice score) of downstream models (nnUNet, SwinUNETR)

results.value: LeFusion-generated data significantly improves the performance of state-of-the-art segmentation models (quantitative values not specified in provided text)

novelty_claims: First lesion-focused diffusion model that explicitly separates lesion synthesis from background, preserving high-fidelity backgrounds.
Redesign of diffusion learning objectives to focus solely on lesion areas, simplifying the learning process and improving control.
Introduction of histogram-based texture control for multi-peak lesion synthesis, enabling control over lesion types during inference.
Proposal of multi-channel decomposition for joint modeling of multi-class lesions, allowing simultaneous generation of multiple lesion classes.
Incorporation of lesion mask diffusion for fine control over lesion size, location, and boundary, increasing diversity.
Demonstration that LeFusion-generated data significantly enhances downstream segmentation model performance.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["FreeTumor: a three-stage framework for tumor synthesis and segmentation leveraging large-scale unlabeled data","Adversarial training strategy to leverage large-scale and diversified unlabeled data in tumor synthesis","GAN-based tumor synthesis model with a segmentation-based discriminator to filter low-quality synthetic tumors","Online synthesis of artificial tumors during segmentation model training","Scaling up tumor segmentation training to 11k cases"],"problems":["Lack of annotated tumor cases for training AI-driven tumor segmentation models","Limited generalization of existing tumor synthesis methods due to reliance on small-scale labeled data","Negative impact of low-quality synthetic tumors on segmentation training","Ineffective use of large-scale unlabeled data in tumor synthesis and segmentation"],"datasets":["LiTs","MSD-Pancreas","KiTs","FLARE23 leaderboard dataset","11k synthetic tumor dataset (0.9k labeled + 10k unlabeled)"],"metrics":["DSC (Dice Similarity Coefficient)"],"results":[{"metric":"DSC (vs. baseline with only real tumors)","value":"+8.9%"},{"metric":"DSC (vs. state-of-the-art DiffTumor)","value":"+6.6%"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes FreeTumor, a simple yet effective annotation-free tumor synthesis and segmentation framework leveraging large-scale unlabeled data","Introduces adversarial training to utilize large-scale and diversified unlabeled data for tumor synthesis, improving generalization","Employs a segmentation-based discriminator to automatically filter out low-quality synthetic tumors, alleviating their negative impact on segmentation training","Scales up tumor segmentation training to 11k cases, the largest dataset for tumor synthesis and segmentation to date","Demonstrates significant improvements over previous methods and releases the 11k synthetic tumor dataset to the community"]}

methods: FreeTumor: a three-stage framework for tumor synthesis and segmentation leveraging large-scale unlabeled data
Adversarial training strategy to leverage large-scale and diversified unlabeled data in tumor synthesis
GAN-based tumor synthesis model with a segmentation-based discriminator to filter low-quality synthetic tumors

novelty_claims: Proposes FreeTumor, a simple yet effective annotation-free tumor synthesis and segmentation framework leveraging large-scale unlabeled data
Introduces adversarial training to utilize large-scale and diversified unlabeled data for tumor synthesis, improving generalization
Employs a segmentation-based discriminator to automatically filter out low-quality synthetic tumors, alleviating their negative impact on segmentation training

Demonstrates significant improvements over previous methods and releases the 11k synthetic tumor dataset to the community

Introduces prediction-guided sample refinement using a pre-trained segmentation model to improve mask-image consistency
Demonstrates that generated data from ArSDM significantly boosts downstream segmentation and detection performance
Enables large-scale generation of annotated or unannotated colonoscopy images for pre-training and analysis

methods: DR-GAN: a conditional generative adversarial network for synthesizing high-resolution fundus images with controllable lesion and grading information
Retina image generator conditioned on vessel, optic disk, and lesion masks, as well as adaptive grading vectors
Multi-scale spatial and channel attention module in the generator to enhance fine details
Multi-scale discriminators with adversarial, feature matching, and grading classification losses
Adaptive grading manipulation module for controlling DR severity in generated images
problems: Imbalanced and insufficient data for high-severity diabetic retinopathy (DR) levels in fundus image datasets
Limited diversity in data augmentation for DR grading and lesion segmentation models
Need for realistic, high-resolution, and controllable synthetic fundus images for data augmentation
datasets: Kaggle EyePACS
FGADR
metrics: True Positive Rate per class for DR grading
novelty_claims: Proposes a conditional high-resolution image generator (DR-GAN) that synthesizes retina images with controllable lesion and grading information, including fine local details via multi-scale spatial and channel attention.
Introduces adaptive grading vectors sampled from learned latent grading spaces to manipulate the severity of synthesized DR images.
Incorporates multi-scale discriminators optimized with adversarial, feature matching, and grading classification losses for improved synthesis quality.
Adds optic disk masks and new lesion types (laser marks, proliferate membranes) as conditioning inputs, improving realism and clinical relevance of generated images.
Utilizes a newly proposed large pixel-level annotated dataset (FGADR) for training and evaluation, demonstrating improved performance.
Provides extensive evaluation including human assessment, FID, SWD, and DR grading accuracy, showing the generated images are both realistic and beneficial for downstream tasks.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Class-aware adversarial synthesis framework for lung nodule generation in CT images","Coarse-to-fine patch in-painter (generator)","Two class-aware discriminators (local and global)","Conditioning on random latent variables and target nodule labels for diverse synthesis","Adversarial learning with L1 reconstruction loss and adversarial losses"],"problems":["Limited size and class imbalance in medical imaging datasets, especially for lung nodules in CT images","Need for effective data augmentation to improve supervised learning tasks such as nodule malignancy classification","Underrepresentation of rare and abnormal cases in training data"],"datasets":["LIDC-IDRI (Lung Image Database Consortium Image Collection)"],"metrics":["Mean AUC (Area Under the Curve) classification score"],"results":[{"metric":"Mean AUC classification score improvement","value":"2% increase when combining real and synthetic lung nodule patches in training"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a class-aware adversarial synthesis framework that can generate lung nodules in CT images with controllable attributes (category and size) at random locations","Introduces a coarse-to-fine generator and two class-aware discriminators for improved synthesis quality and class conditioning","Demonstrates that augmenting training data with synthesized nodules improves malignancy classification performance across different network architectures","Shows the potential for generalizing the framework to synthesize other objects of interest in medical images"]}

methods: Class-aware adversarial synthesis framework for lung nodule generation in CT images
Coarse-to-fine patch in-painter (generator)
Two class-aware discriminators (local and global)
Conditioning on random latent variables and target nodule labels for diverse synthesis
Adversarial learning with L1 reconstruction loss and adversarial losses
problems: Limited size and class imbalance in medical imaging datasets, especially for lung nodules in CT images
Need for effective data augmentation to improve supervised learning tasks such as nodule malignancy classification
Underrepresentation of rare and abnormal cases in training data
datasets: LIDC-IDRI (Lung Image Database Consortium Image Collection)
metrics: Mean AUC (Area Under the Curve) classification score

results.value: 2% increase when combining real and synthetic lung nodule patches in training

novelty_claims: Proposes a class-aware adversarial synthesis framework that can generate lung nodules in CT images with controllable attributes (category and size) at random locations
Introduces a coarse-to-fine generator and two class-aware discriminators for improved synthesis quality and class conditioning
Demonstrates that augmenting training data with synthesized nodules improves malignancy classification performance across different network architectures
Shows the potential for generalizing the framework to synthesize other objects of interest in medical images

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Mask conditional diffusion model (MaC-DM) for generating synthetic radiographic images with and without CMLs","Blinded radiologist assessment of images (diagnosis of CML and real vs synthetic discrimination)","t-distributed stochastic neighbor embedding (t-SNE) for data distribution analysis"],"problems":["Limited size and diversity of training datasets for automated deep-learning detection of classic metaphyseal lesion (CML) in infants","Need for objective and subjective assessment of the realism and diagnostic utility of synthetic radiographic images"],"datasets":["Existing distal tibia dataset (177 normal, 73 with CMLs)","Randomly selected test set of 100 real images (50 normal, 50 with CMLs)","100 synthetic images generated by MaC-DM (50 normal, 50 with CMLs)"],"metrics":["Kappa statistic (inter-rater agreement)","Accuracy","Area under the curve (AUC) from t-SNE analysis"],"results":[{"metric":"Kappa (diagnosis of CMLs)","value":"0.90 (95% CI: 0.88-0.92)"},{"metric":"Accuracy (diagnosis of CMLs)","value":"92% (95% CI: 89-97)"},{"metric":"Kappa (real vs synthetic discrimination)","value":"0.05 (95% CI: 0.03-0.07)"},{"metric":"Accuracy (real vs synthetic discrimination)","value":"53% (95% CI: 49-59)"},{"metric":"AUC (t-SNE, normal vs CML)","value":"0.996 (95% CI: 0.992-1.000, P<.01)"},{"metric":"AUC (t-SNE, real vs synthetic)","value":"0.566 (95% CI: 0.486-0.647, P=.11)"}],"novelty_claims":["First use of a mask conditional diffusion model (MaC-DM) to generate synthetic radiographic images of distal tibial CMLs in infants.","Demonstrates that synthetic images generated by MaC-DM are indistinguishable from real images by expert radiologists.","Shows potential of MaC-DM for effective data augmentation in training machine-learning models for CML diagnosis."]}

methods: Mask conditional diffusion model (MaC-DM) for generating synthetic radiographic images with and without CMLs
Blinded radiologist assessment of images (diagnosis of CML and real vs synthetic discrimination)
t-distributed stochastic neighbor embedding (t-SNE) for data distribution analysis
problems: Limited size and diversity of training datasets for automated deep-learning detection of classic metaphyseal lesion (CML) in infants
Need for objective and subjective assessment of the realism and diagnostic utility of synthetic radiographic images
datasets: Existing distal tibia dataset (177 normal, 73 with CMLs)
Randomly selected test set of 100 real images (50 normal, 50 with CMLs)
100 synthetic images generated by MaC-DM (50 normal, 50 with CMLs)
metrics: Area under the curve (AUC) from t-SNE analysis
novelty_claims: First use of a mask conditional diffusion model (MaC-DM) to generate synthetic radiographic images of distal tibial CMLs in infants.
Demonstrates that synthetic images generated by MaC-DM are indistinguishable from real images by expert radiologists.
Shows potential of MaC-DM for effective data augmentation in training machine-learning models for CML diagnosis.

methods: Unsupervised learning-based generative adversarial network with adaptive normalization (AN-GAN)
Extraction of deep semantic information from high-frequency components of DWI images
Fusion of semantic information as a modality mask vector into deconvolution layers
Introduction of adaptive normalization (AN), a conditional normalization layer modulated by the fused feature map
problems: Synthesizing T2-weighted MR images from diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) MR images
Reducing reliance on labeled datasets for MR image synthesis
Improving perceptual quality and detail in synthesized MR images
novelty_claims: Proposes an unsupervised GAN-based method (AN-GAN) for T2 synthesis from DWI without requiring labeled datasets
Extracts and fuses deep semantic information from high-frequency image components as a modality mask vector to guide GAN training
Introduces adaptive normalization (AN), a conditional normalization layer modulated by the fused feature map, to better preserve semantic information compared to common normalization layers
Demonstrates improved perceptual quality and detail in synthesized T2 images compared to state-of-the-art methods
